stem: A physics class is studying a model rocket. The equation y = -16(x - 4.2)2 + 380 gives the estimated height y, in feet, of the model rocket x seconds after it is launched. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of the equation in the xy-plane?

answerOptions.content: The rocket reaches an estimated maximum height of 380 feet 16 seconds after it is launched.
The rocket reaches an estimated maximum height of 16 feet 380 seconds after it is launched.
The rocket reaches an estimated maximum height of 380 feet 4.2 seconds after it is launched.
The rocket reaches an estimated maximum height of 4.2 feet 380 seconds after it is launched.

rationale: Choice C is correct. When a quadratic is written as y = a(x - h)2 + k, the vertex of its graph is (h, k). Here h = 4.2 and k = 380, and since the coefficient -16 is negative, the parabola opens downward and the vertex is the maximum point. So the rocket reaches a maximum height of 380 feet 4.2 seconds after launch. The other choices swap the coordinates of the vertex or use the coefficient -16.
